frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre bhai, aajkal bahut confusion hai in logon ka. Itna makeup karengi to lagega asli chehra chhupa rahi hain, aur bilkul dhyan na do to bolenge apne aap ko maintain hi nahi karti. Seedhi si baat hai, natural raho aur thoda presentable raho bas, itna drama kis liye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: criticizing 'too much makeup' as deceptive while also expecting women to stay 'presentable.' It encodes the makeup paradox and frames women's grooming as something to be judged and regulated by men.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
